source.title: An integrative theory of prefrontal cortex function

confidence_rationale: Miller & Cohen 2001 is one of the most cited papers in cognitive neuroscience (~8000+ citations). The core claim -- that PFC maintains goal and context representations that provide top-down bias signals to downstream areas -- is precisely the frontal cue gating mechanism in Topic 3. Source quality exceptional. Mapping fidelity very high: the 'bias signal' model is the REE ARC-016 claim about PFC providing context that shapes hippocampal retrieval.
failure_signatures: The original formulation emphasises sensory and motor routing, less explicitly the hippocampal memory retrieval pathway -- the extension to hippocampal option retrieval requires citing subsequent work (Jadhav, Tang).
Lesion and single-unit data available in 2001 were largely from non-human primates on working memory tasks; the generality to naturalistic behaviour is assumed.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Cognitive control stems from active maintenance of goal/context patterns in PFC that provide bias signals to downstream brain structures, guiding activity along neural pathways to establish proper input-output mappings for a given task.
mapping.ree_translation: This is the foundational account of ARC-016: PFC's role is not to compute responses but to bias which pathways are active given a goal context. In REE, the 'bias signal' is what selects which hippocampal trajectories are retrieved for evaluation by E3. The Miller/Cohen framework gives the mechanistic vocabulary: goal-active PFC biases the hippocampal pattern-completion attractor toward context-relevant options.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Miller & Cohen 2001 does not explicitly address hippocampal trajectory retrieval -- their model is primarily about sensory-to-motor routing and PFC-cortical projections. The extension to hippocampal sequence retrieval is a downstream inference that has since been supported empirically.
mapping.source_context: Annual Review of Neuroscience; synthesis of single-unit NHP data, neuroimaging, lesion, and computational modelling across attention, working memory, and task switching paradigms.

confidence_components.notes: Canonical source; mapping requires one downstream inference step (PFC bias -> hippocampal retrieval).
